nativeRange.habitat: Tropical and subtropical streamside microhabitats; grows attached to wood, rocks, and tree roots in humid riparian zones, consistent with other Taxiphyllum mosses

decorPreferences: driftwood and branching wood (vertical flame-shaped fronds showcase well on wood)
stainless or plastic mesh for moss walls
tied with thread/fishing line or attached with cyanoacrylate gel until rhizoids anchor
gentle to moderate flow keeps fronds free of debris and accentuates the upward growth

summary: An Asian aquarium moss prized for its distinctive upward, spiraling, flame-shaped growth — a striking contrast to the bushy irregularity of Java Moss and the triangulated fronds of Christmas Moss. Generally advised to attach to wood or rock as an epiphyte; tolerates a wide range of light and water conditions.
careNotes: Taxiphyllum sp. 'Flame' is a trade-designated Asian moss in the family Hypnaceae (order Hypnales), closely related to its better-known siblings Java Moss (Taxiphyllum barbieri, fw-plant-033) and Christmas Moss (Vesicularia montagnei, fw-plant-114). It is most easily distinguished by its UPWARD, SPIRALING, FLAME-SHAPED growth — fronds twist gently as they ascend, giving the appearance of a deep green campfire, which is the trait commonly highlighted by aquarium retailers. ATTACHMENT: like other aquarium mosses, Flame Moss is an epiphyte — tie or glue it to driftwood, porous rock, lava rock, or plastic/stainless mesh; do NOT bury it in substrate. Rhizoids anchor the moss to the surface over several weeks. LIGHTING is generally tolerant of low to medium levels; the moss survives in low-light setups, but moderate light encourages denser, fuller, and more strongly vertical growth. CO2 is optional — the plant grows without supplementation but tends to grow faster and denser with CO2 injection. GROWTH RATE is reported as slow to moderate across sources, consistent with most aquarium mosses; trimming controls shape. TEMPERATURE tolerance is broadly tropical (around 20–28 °C / 68–82 °F); pH tolerance is wide (sources span 6.0–8.0), with 6.0–7.5 being a sensible aquarium target. FERTILIZATION demand is low — the moss absorbs nutrients from the water column and does not require a nutrient-rich substrate, though weekly liquid fertilization has been reported by hobbyists to encourage fuller growth. SHRIMP AND FRY REFUGE: the upright fronds form excellent microhabitat for newly hatched fry and for dwarf-shrimp larvae, which graze biofilm and microfauna from the moss. PARAMETER SPREAD ACROSS SOURCES: Buce Plant reports pH 6–8, low-to-medium light, CO2 optional but recommended; Aquarium Co-Op reports 5–15 cm height, slow horizontal spread, easy care, and notes the 'Asian Flame Moss' trade name; Aquariumplants.com reports temperature 20–28 °C, pH 6.0–7.5, low to moderate light, CO2 optional, 2–5 cm tall in aquariums forming dense clumps. Height range was averaged toward the larger end (5–15 cm) since multiple sources note vertical fronds elongate visibly under decent light. SOURCING NOTE: Wikipedia covers the genus Taxiphyllum but does not have a dedicated page for the 'Flame' trade designation; only the Java Moss species (T. barbieri) is documented there. The 'Flame' moss is widely regarded as an undescribed Asian Taxiphyllum species circulated in the aquarium trade.
breedingNotes: Vegetative propagation only in aquarium conditions: cut or rip off any healthy portion of the mat and re-attach it to a new surface — every fragment regrows. There is no need to wait for any reproductive cycle. True sexual reproduction (spore production from a sporophyte) requires emersed growth and is essentially never observed in submerged aquarium tanks.

primary.name: Buce Plant — Flame Moss
primary.notes: Primary product page. Used for: scientific name (Taxiphyllum 'Flame'), care level (easy), pH 6–8, lighting (low to medium), CO2 not necessary but recommended, propagation by 'cut and rip', epiphyte placement (can be attached to hardscape or left free-floating), and warnings against drastic parameter changes (which can cause melt and rot). Notes that growth and coloration improve with CO2 injection.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op — Taxiphyllum sp. 'Flame Moss' Tissue Culture
additional.notes: Secondary cross-verification source. Used for: scientific name confirmation (Taxiphyllum sp. 'Flame Moss'), trade synonym 'Asian Flame Moss', height range 5–15 cm, slow horizontal spread, vertical undulating growth pattern (described as 'a deep green campfire'), easy care level, and propagation by dividing portions. Tissue-culture product, so no fertilizer/parameter specifics on the page itself.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Taxiphyllum
additional.notes: Taxonomic authority. Used for: confirmation of family Hypnaceae and order Hypnales, genus description as 'mat-forming' with 'glossy and creeping' stems, and genus distribution covering Asia (India, Japan, Philippines among others). Wikipedia does not have a dedicated entry for the 'Flame' trade designation — only Java Moss (T. barbieri) is documented as an economically important aquarium species.

additional.name: Aquariumplants.com — Flame Moss
additional.notes: Third cross-verification source for water parameters. Used for: temperature 20–28 °C (68–82 °F), pH 6.0–7.5, low to moderate lighting, CO2 optional but accelerates growth, slow to moderate growth rate, dense low clump form (2–5 cm in their reading), foreground/midground placement. Confirms the species is 'often listed as Taxiphyllum sp.' with exact species uncertain — corroborating the undescribed-trade-species status.

propagation: cut and rip — divide and re-attach to new surfaces
